definition: EBITDA addback practices (15-40%) in >90% covenant-lite loans systematically overstate reported EBITDA, meaning a firm reporting 4.0x leverage may be running 5.5-6.5x on a cash-flow-consistent basis. This corrupts accounting-based distress factors (Z-score, O-score, distance-to-default) at the input level.
evidence: >90% covenant-lite with 15-40% addbacks is observable market structure
Reported EBITDA systematically overstated as denominator in leverage and coverage ratios
Corrupts accounting-based distress factors at the input level
open_questions: How much of the signal corruption survives in market-implied distress measures (CDS-implied PD, option-adjusted spreads)?
Can alternative cash-flow-consistent metrics replace corrupted accounting-based factors?
What is the aggregate magnitude of leverage understatement across the market?
